hook: AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-liner. Again.
caption:
AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-liner. Again.

Here's what's actually happening:

ChatGPT doesn't know if you're a "give me the full context" person or a "bottom line first, details never" person.

So it guesses. And it guesses long. Because long feels thorough. Thorough feels safe.

But you? You asked "should I take the meeting?" and got a 400-word breakdown of stakeholder dynamics.

The fix isn't reprompting every single time with "be concise" like you're scolding a golden retriever.

The fix is an AI that already knows you process information in bullet points. That knows you hate throat-clearing. That knows "just tell me the answer" isn't impatience — it's literally how your brain works.

That's what a BootFile does. It's a profile built around how YOU think, so the AI responds in your format by default.

No more "be concise." No more "shorter please." No more third attempt at getting a usable answer.

Just... the response you needed the first time.
